data.title: Renderer pool to lift the 1-render-at-a-time ceiling

data.body:
Severity: performance high. Source: TYB-12-followup performance audit.

Where: server/internal/render/render.go:35-115. The Renderer struct holds a single *resvg.Renderer behind a single sync.Mutex; every RenderPNG call holds the mutex start-to-finish.

What: resvg-go wasm is non-concurrent; the project today has exactly one instance. Renders are 0.5-2s on x86_64 wasm for 3600x4800 PNGs. The render path is on the critical path of /api/render AND /api/checkout/start, so the entire site is capped at ~1 req/s aggregate.

Impact: any concurrent buyer queues behind the renderer mutex; under 50 unique designs in a burst (singleflight only collapses identical hashes), tail latency = 50× single-render time. The 60s WriteTimeout is the only thing keeping the queue from growing unbounded; once a request times out mid-render, the wasm work still completes (no ctx propagation) — wasted cycles.

Recommendation: build a pool of N *resvg.Renderer instances behind a buffered semaphore channel. Configurable via RENDERER_POOL_SIZE env var; default to GOMAXPROCS or 4. The package comment at render.go:35-36 already pre-approves this direction. Effort: small (50-100 LoC). Even N=4 raises the ceiling 4×.

Notes for planning: resvg-go v0.0.1's Close() is documented as broken — the pool design should share the *resvg.Context but allocate per-instance Renderers; investigate carefully. Pairs naturally with TYB-20 (per-call timeout).

Implemented the renderer pool per the plan, no scope deviations.

Summary:
- render.go: replaced single Renderer + sync.Mutex with N resvg instances behind a buffered channel; each instance owns its own resvg.Context (one wazero runtime per slot, the only safe config given v0.0.1's per-Context wasm linear memory). RenderPNG takes (ctx, in); the acquire-side ctx select gives cancelled clients a fast path. Wasm call is intentionally not ctx-aware (TYB-20). (*resvg.Renderer).Close still skipped (broken in v0.0.1).
- main.go: RENDERER_POOL_SIZE env var, default 4, invalid/non-positive clamps to 1 with a warning, logs 'renderer pool size: N' (and the no-parallelism hint when N=1). Doc-comment env section updated.
- 3 prod call sites + 3 test sites updated for the (ctx, in) signature.
- Tests: parallel-renders-up-to-size, acquire-side ctx cancellation, size clamping (0/-3 -> 1). Existing golden test still passes byte-for-byte. Used a package-private renderFn closure so parallelism tests don't need a real wasm runtime.
- .env.example + README updated; README architecture bullet rewritten so it no longer says renders are serialised through a mutex.

Commits:
- 3eb2d8a TYB-16: refactor(render): pool resvg renderers behind a buffered channel
- c76b6b6 TYB-16: docs: document RENDERER_POOL_SIZE and the render pool

Verification:
- go build ./..., go vet ./..., go test ./... all green from server/.
- Manual smoke (DATA_DIR=/tmp/tyb-smoke-pool*, RENDERER_POOL_SIZE varied):
  - Startup logs: 'renderer pool size: 4' for default; 'renderer pool size: 1' + the parallelism hint when set to 1; warning lines on RENDERER_POOL_SIZE=foo and =-3 with clamp to 1.
  - 6 concurrent /api/render with pool=4: 2.37s wall (vs 0.92s single-render baseline). Roughly ceil(6/4) x baseline = 1.84s; observed slightly higher with curl/connect overhead.
  - Same burst with pool=1: 4.36s wall — pool is the differentiator.

No flags for the reviewer.

Verdict: PASS.

What was reviewed: commits 3eb2d8a + c76b6b6 on server-setup (pool implementation + docs). Read full diff, all touched files, ran go vet + go test (cached + forced rerun on TestPool*), and 4 manual smoke scenarios.

Findings: none blocking. One minor cosmetic note — when poolSize == 1, main.go:94-97 emits two log lines that both start with 'renderer pool size: 1' (the generic line then the parallelism-hint line). Slightly redundant; trivial to compress to one line in a follow-up if it bugs you.

Acceptance criteria walkthrough:
1. PASS pool=4 parallel renders confirmed via TestPool_ParallelRendersUpToSize (high-water counter asserts ==2 with size=2) and live smoke (6 concurrent at 1.75s vs 4.7s with pool=1; 2.7x throughput).
2. PASS N=4 + 5 concurrent: pool channel naturally blocks the 5th; TestPool_ParallelRendersUpToSize covers the analogous N=2 + 3-concurrent case.
3. PASS Default 4 confirmed from main.go:177-180 + smoke startup log.
4. PASS RENDERER_POOL_SIZE=foo clamps to 1 with warning (smoke verified live).
5. PASS Acquire-side ctx cancellation: TestPool_AcquireContextCancellation asserts ctx.Canceled in <50ms with all slots held.
6. PASS Wasm call is NOT ctx-aware: render.go:180 calls renderFn(inst, svg) without ctx; comment at 150-151 explicitly defers to TYB-20.
7. PASS Existing golden test passes against NewRenderer(ctx, 1); golden bytes unchanged.
8. PASS go test ./... and go vet ./... green from server/.
9. PASS README env-var section, .env.example, and main.go package doc all updated.
10. PASS Close() iterates insts and closes each ctx; idempotent via nil-check at top.

Other correctness checks:
- PASS One Context per instance (render.go:92-110 loops resvg.NewContext N times).
- PASS WOFF->TTF runs once at top (render.go:81); same []byte passed to every LoadFontData.
- PASS Buffered channel pool (chan *instance), not sync.Pool.
- PASS Partial-failure unwind via closePartial closure (render.go:87-91).
- PASS (*resvg.Renderer).Close intentionally not called; file header documents the v0.0.1 bug.
- PASS Three production caller sites pass r.Context(): handlers.go:110, checkout_handlers.go:154, printful_handlers.go:162 + 366.
- PASS Commit prefixes both TYB-16:; no emoji; no --no-verify; no scope creep.
